embeds.title: Introduction
embeds.description:
DiceCloud is a completely free solution to creating and managing character sheets. It utilizes a container method of creating features, while using a programmatic lite approach to calculate the values that make up a character sheet. Because of this more do-it-yourself style of creating a sheet, DiceCloud is **NOT recommended for beginners** or those who dislike technically manual work. DiceCloud is ideal for individuals who don't mind putting in a little extra work, do not have or do not wish to spend the money on D&D Beyond, or wish to use UA and Homebrew material (elsewhere, as this server does not permit the use of homebrew).

So without further ado, let's get to it!

fields.name: Note
fields.value: This walkthrough is focused on DiceCloud v1, which is powerful but limited in what is readily provided. It is recommended to use the updated (v2) DiceCloud. See our <#1247340452156539021> for DiceCloud v2.

fields.name: Special Thanks & Resources
fields.value: A huge thanks to <@658201358247264257> for writing the first version of this guide for the server! This revision was written from that walkthrough, the [official guide](https://v1.dicecloud.com/guide)

embeds.title: Creating a Character
embeds.description:
To get started:
- Head to https://v1.DiceCloud.com/ and sign in (or create your account)
- Select the `Characters` menu option
- Select the `(+)` button in the lower-right corner
- Select `New Character` from the pop-up menu.
- Give your character a Name, Gender, and Race

For demonstration purposes, I've chosen a Tiefling

fields.name: Stats
fields.value: The `Stats` tab shows your current HP, core Ability scores, skill proficiencies, saving throws, armor class, etc.

fields.name: Features
fields.value: The `Features` tab is where you'll enter your race and class features, your armor, tool, and weapon proficiencies, along with your attacks.

fields.name: Inventory
fields.value: The `Inventory` tab shows all of your equipment, handling which items are equipped and magic items attuned. Note, however, that the Avrae bot __will not__ import your inventory. We track it in-server using the `!bag` command.

fields.name: Spells
fields.value: The `Spells` tab will hold the list of spells your character knows and can prepare. For purely martial characters, this tab can be disabled in the character settings.

fields.name: Persona
fields.value: The `Persona` tab contains your character's Background and Language Proficiencies, as well as other character details such as personality and physical description.

fields.name: Journal
fields.value: The `Journal` tab manages your race, class and level. You can use it to track your experience (though we use the `!xp` alias in-server for that). You can also add notes to this tab.

embeds.title: 1️⃣ Set Ability Scores
embeds.description:
Your character's ability scores are all set to 10 by default, so you'll need to update them with the values that you rolled in <#697021317727387718>, or that came from your Point Buy. Enter exactly the base values, __without your racial bonuses__. We'll apply those in the next step
- Navigate to the `Features` tab
- Select the Base Ability Scores feature
- Click the pencil icon in the top right corner
- Click the pencil icon to the right of your character's first ability score
- Make sure `Base Value` is selected.
- Input your character's base value for that stat (__without__ any racial ASI)
- Repeat for the rest of your ability scores

For the purposes of our demo, I'll use a Standard Array

embeds.title: 2️⃣ Racial Details
embeds.description:
When creating the character, you are prompted to enter a Race for them. However, DiceCloud doesn't automatically populate the racial details, so you will have to do that manually by following these steps.
- Click the `Journal` tab (in the top row menu, on the far right)
- Click the race you entered to open the racial dialog box.
 - For our example, I'll enter `Tiefling`
- Click the pencil icon in the top corner of the dialog to edit
Here, you can change your race or and add the effects and proficiencies your chosen race gives you.

fields.name: Ability Score Increases
fields.value:
- Click the `Add Effect` button
- Select `Ability Scores` in the left menu, and the ability to apply the racial bonus
 - For the purposes of our demo, I'll select Charisma.
- Choose `Add`, in the right column (this should be the default).
- Input the value for your first ASI (for our demo, `+2`)
- Click the return arrow in the upper left to finalize it.
- Repeat the steps for any further ASIs (where applicable).

fields.name: Speed
fields.value:
- Click the `Add Effect` button; the Effect window will open.
- In the left menu, scroll down to `Stats` and choose `Speed`.
- Choose `Base Value`, in the right menu.
- Input the value for your race's base speed (usually 30, but it might vary)
- Click the return arrow in the upper left to finalize it.

fields.name: Proficiencies
fields.value:
- Click the `Add Proficiency` button. A line will pop up to add your proficiency.
- Select the type of proficiency from the leftmost dropdown. This will let you select a Skill, Saving Throw, Weapon, Armor, Tool, or Language proficiency.
- The center column will change to either a dropdown or textbox depending on the type of proficiency selected. In our example, we'll enter the language.
- The rightmost dropdown sets the Proficiency level. Other types include Half proficient (for Bard Jack of All Trades) and Double-proficient (for Expertise).
- Repeat these steps for all proficiencies your Race gives you.

embeds.title: Racial Features
embeds.description:
While many other racial traits and features can be added in the racial dialog (for instance, our Tiefling's Fire Resistance) it is preferrable to add these features to the appropriately-named `Features` tab.
- Navigate to the `Features` tab, and click the button to add a new feature.
- Enter the name of the feature. In our Tiefling example, `Hellish Resistance`
- Click a button to add an effect, proficiency, attack, or buff, where appropriate. In our example, we'll select `Add Effect`, select `Weakness/Resistance`, and select `Fire` and `Resistance`
- (Optional) By clicking on the paintbucket icon, you can color-code your entered features to indicate their source.
- Repeat this process for all other racial features. For our example Tiefling, we can add Darkvision and Infernal Legacy without applying an effect. We'll cover racial spells (such as those granted by Infernal Legacy) later in the walkthrough.

fields.name: Enable Feature
fields.value: The `Enable Feature` setting allows you to determine whether an effect is always applied, as well as whether it is enabled or disabled by default. This allows you to set up stat adjustments for features which are conditionally applied or activated. None of our features are conditional yet, so for now just leave this set to Always Enabled.

fields.name: Limited Uses
fields.value: The `Limited Uses` setting allows you to specify a number of uses for a feature which will reset on a rest. This will automatically create a counter in Avrae which will allow you to keep track of how many times you can use the ability as you play. If your ability description contains the phrase `short rest` or `long rest`, the counter will reset with the appropriate rest. For our example, we will leave this off.

embeds.title: 3️⃣ Class Details
embeds.description:
Return to the `Journal` tab for your character. Just above where you set your race, you'll notice a field that reads `Level 0`.
- Click the (`+`) sign beside the `Level 0` text to bring up the new Class dialog.
- Enter the name of your base class and starting level.
 - For our demo, we'll choose `Sorcerer` and start at level 1 (in order to demonstrate Multiclassing)
- Enter the following class details:

fields.name: Proficiencies
fields.value:
- Click the `Add Proficiency` button
- Click the dropdown box that currently has `Skill` selected, and choose `Saving Throw` instead
- In the `Proficiency` dropdown, select the first saving throw provided by your class
- Leave the third dropdown as `Proficient`
- Repeat these steps for your the second Saving Throw proficiency provided by your class
- Repeat these steps for skill proficiencies, as well as any Armor, Weapon, or Tool proficiencies your class provides

fields.name: Hit Points
fields.value:
We don't want to enter our hit point value manually. Instead, we'll use a formula to calculate the fixed hit points. DiceCloud calculates equations dynamically in any field that has a light bulb icon in it. The variable used to find the level of a class is always `<class>Level`, where <class> is the appropriate class name. Avrae will pick this up too for aliasing! So always name your class properly.
- Click the `Add Effect` button
- Select `Stats`, then `Hit Points` on the left-hand column
- Select `Base Value` for your first class, or `Add` for a multiclass.
- In the `Value` field, enter your formula.
 - (Fixed HP amount x your <class>Level + the difference between your 1st Level HP)
 - For example, a Sorcerer has 6 HP at level 1, and a fixed 4 HP every level after. So I'll set this up as `(SorcererLevel * 4) + 2`
- **Note**: DiceCloud dynamically adds your constitution modifier to your hit point total, so __do not include it in the formula__.

fields.name: Hit Dice
fields.value:
- Click the Add Effect button
- Select `Hit Dice`, and choose the appropriate Hit Die size for your class
 - For our example, Sorcerers get d6 hit dice.
- Choose `Base Value` (or `Add` for a multiclass)
- Enter `<class>Level`
 - Replace `<class>` with your appropriate class name. For our example, I would use `SorcererLevel`

fields.name: Multiclassing
fields.value:
If you choose to multiclass, you will need to repeat these steps to create a Class card for each class with a few exceptions
- Be sure to double check what your second class provides when multiclassing, as some specific cases apply.
- Remember to only add the Proficiencies from your first class, unless your subsequent classes explicitly grant them on multiclassing.
- For Hit Points, choose `Add` instead of `Base Value`, and the formula should be the Fixed Value * <class>Level.
- Hit Dice should `Add` instead of using `Base Value` as well.

embeds.title: Class Features
embeds.description:
Much like our racial features, we will add our Class features manually.
- Navigate to the `Features` tab, and click the button to add a new feature.
- Enter the name and description of the feature.
 - In our example, we'll add the `Spellcasting` feature. The description of this feature is rather long, but copying the entire thing to have it as a reference is helpful.
- Note that the Description field allows for Markdown (like Discord posts) and formulas to automatically update the description as your character grows in levels.
- Click a button to add an effect, proficiency, attack, or buff, where appropriate.
 - In our example, we'll add our Spell Slots as an Effect, just like Hit Points.
 - Choose `Add Effect`. Select `Spell Slots`, and select the appropriate level (in this case, level 1)
 - Choosing spells will be covered later in the walkthrough.
- (Optional) By clicking on the paintbucket icon, you can color-code your entered features to indicate their source.
- You can repeat this process for all other Class features, including any applicable to your chosen subclass.

embeds.title: 4️⃣ Add a Background
embeds.description:
Once you have selected your background (you can see the backgrounds available, with links to more details, in <#911129455345405992>) you can enter the details by following these steps:
- Navigate back to the `Persona` tab and select the pre-populated `Background` item
- Enter the name of your background
- Add any proficiencies your Background provides
- [Optional] Select and fill out the pre-populated `Personality`, `Bonds`, `Ideals`, and `Flaws` items. While these details are optional - they do nothing to your character mechanically - they can be very helpful in fleshing them out to be more than a collection of attributes.

fields.name: Background Feature
fields.value:
Just like race and class features, you can add your Background feature in the `Features` tab.
- Navigate to the `Features` tab, and click the button to add a new feature.
- Enter the name and description of the feature.
Note that Background features generally don't provide a mechanical benefit, and don't have any Effects or Proficiencies to add. Features which provide access to a Feat are not approved for use. Ravnica backgrounds, while approved, do NOT grant the additional spell lists.

embeds.title: 5️⃣ Spells [Optional]
embeds.description:
If your chosen race or class has a feature which allows your character to cast spells you will need to enter the spells you know to populate your sheet in Avrae.
- Navigate to the `Spells` tab and click on the (`+`) button in the lower-right. This will popup a sub-menu with three options (from top to bottom)
 - `New Spell` A blank spell that you can fill in the fields to create a spell from scratch. (You will need a Spell List first).
 - `Spell Library` allows you to quickly and easily add spells from the SRD to your character sheet.
 - `New Spell List` creates a new container for your spells. This is required. The Spell List dictates your spell attack bonus and spell save DC

embeds.title: Create Spell List(s)
embeds.description:
Begin by creating a new spell list.
- Click the (`+`) button, followed by the `Spell List` button
- Enter the name of your spell list
- Change `intelligenceMod` to the appropriate spellcasting Ability for this list.
- Enter the Maximum Prepared Spells. Note that this is a Formula field to allow you to calculate it based on your applicable spellcasting modifier and class levels, as appropriate.
- Enter a description if you like.

**Note**: DiceCloud defaults to adding new spells to whichever spell list was created **first**, which can be annoying if you have multiple classes or races that each provide their own spellcasting. It is recommended to create any relevant __Class__ spell lists first, before any __Race__ spell lists. In the case of multiclassed characters, create a list for whichever class you anticipate will be your highest level class. Alternately, if all sources use the same Spell Save DC and Attack Bonus, you may instead choose to put them all in a single list and color-code individual spells to show their source.

embeds.title: Select SRD Spells
embeds.description:
The exact number and levels of spells, and the spell lists available to you will depend on your class. Check the rules for your class for how many spells you can learn, and what spells are available to you.
- Click the (`+`) button
- Choose the `Spell from Library` button
- Dropdown for Cantrips, and check the appropriate boxes
- Repeat for any other spell levels you can cast

**Note**: DiceCloud will NOT filter SRD spells based on class, so you will want to double-check that the spells you are selecting are available to your chosen class.

fields.name: Changling Lists
fields.value:
DiceCloud doesn't allow you to specify the target list when you're adding library spells. If you are adding to multiple lists, you may need to change which list a spell is assigned under:
- Click the spell in the list and the `Edit` pencil icon
- Click the `Spell List` dropdown and select the appropriate list.

embeds.title: Create Non-SRD Spells
embeds.description:
DiceCloud only includes content provided by Wizards of the Coast in the official system reference document. If the spell you are looking for is not available in the system reference document, you will need to add it manually.
- Click the (`+`) button and the `Spell` button
- Enter the details of the spell (Name, Level, Spell School, Casting time, Range, Components, Duration, Description)
 - Be sure to select the appropriate Spell List if you have more than one.
- Add an Attack or a Buff to the spell, as appropriate. Note that for the most part, this step will be unnecessary - the server has automation enabled for most spells.

footer.text: Creating an attack for a non-SRD Spell

embeds.title: Preparing Spells
embeds.description: DiceCloud has support for tracking your Prepared Spells. In the top-right corner of the spell list, you'll see a bookmark icon. If you click that, you will see checkmarks next to all of your spells in that list - you can use this to set whether the spell is prepared or not. If you click the checkmark again, any unprepared spells will disappear, so you always have your list of prepared spells ready for use.

embeds.title: 6️⃣ Equipment & Attacks
embeds.description:
Characters begin with their starting equipment and 150 gold. You can see what equipment you start with by visiting the page for your class (linked in <#911129455345405992>) and find your starting equipment on [5e.tools](https://5e.tools/classes.html). Avrae **does not** sync your equipment from DiceCloud, so you will need to do that by hand with the bot using the `!bag` and `!coins` aliases. Therefore, it isn't necessary to enter most of your equipment. However, it is recommended to do so for anything that provides an attack (weapons)  or affects your AC (armor).
- Click the (`+`) button, notice the three sub-options
 - `Item`: A blank, customizable item. You can use this for items not in the library, like trinkets and magic items.
 - `Item from Library`: Any item available in the SRD. Selecting one of these will generate the item's details for you. This is the most convenient, and items created from this may be used as a template and modified to create similar non-SRD items.
 - `Container`: Use this to create an organizational container in your `Inventory` tab.
- Select the `Item from Library` button
- Select the dropdown for Weapons (or Tools, or Armor)... and select the appropriate item
- Repeat for any other items in your starting gear (one item at a time)
 - DiceCloud will create Attacks for any weapons you select in this manner
- Equip the newly added item by dragging it from the `Carried` container into the `Equipment` container. Armor is not worn and attacks from weapons are not shown in Avrae unless they are in the Equipment section.

embeds.title: Modifying Items
embeds.description:
If you have, for example, a pact weapon or a magic item with a +1 bonus, you'll need to set that up in DiceCloud to be able to use in Avrae.
- Click your item to open its container, and click the pencil icon in the top right.
- Scroll down to the Attacks section. Here you can edit the attack bonus and damage. For example, this is what a Flame Tongue would look like:

embeds.title: 7️⃣ Optional Details
embeds.description: The following are optional - they do not have any bearing on your character's mechanics, but can help you flesh out your character.

fields.name: Character Details
fields.value: Clicking on your character's name in the `Persona` tab will allow you to edit their name, alignment, gender, race, and provide a URL to serve as a picture.

fields.name: Experience log
fields.value: This is optional - we track exp on the server via Avrae - but if you want to keep track of it yourself, you can click the `+` button to add a new entry, and you can provide a name, the amount of experience you gained, and any notes about the entry

fields.name: Character Notes
fields.value: Clicking the (`+`) button at the bottom of the `Journal` tab will add a note to the page to keep track of your character's goals or progress.

embeds.title: 8️⃣ Sheet Menu & Settings
embeds.description:
In the upper-right of the character sheet, there is a button with 3 vertical dots. If you click this, you open the options menu with the following options:
- `Delete` - Delete your sheet. Be careful with this.
- `Share` - Open the Sharing settings
- `Setting` - Opens the settings menu
 - `Hide Spells Tab` - If your character can't cast spells, use this to unclutter your sheet by hiding the spells tab

The other options are irrelevant for our purposes, but they are mostly self-explanatory.

embeds.title: 9️⃣ Register Your Sheet with Avrae
embeds.description:
By default, your sheet can be seen by others only if they are explicitly added, so it is necessary to share your sheet so our DMs and Avrae can read it.
- Go into the Sheet Menu by clicking the three dots in the upper-right
- Click the `Share` button
- Change the `Who can view this character` dropdown to `Anyone with link`
- In <#694275490306981947>, enter the command `!import <url>` where <url> is the link to your sheet

fields.name: Avrae Automation
fields.value: A list of Avrae's [currently automated Actions](https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1w66DFqD83hkwXqP7rTYx26QAcy1FJmhATpVIgfLmslM/edit#gid=658150111) for use on GSheet and DiceCloud. By naming your features **exactly** as provided in this spreadsheet, Avrae will automatically create actions for them. __**Note**__: Due to Avrae being owned by Wizards of the Coast, any non-SRD actions will be inaccessible if you do not have the relevant source material purchased in DnD Beyond. That is something we will be working to rectify in the future.

fields.name: DiceCloud Helper
fields.value: [This Google sheet](https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1UfZ9e9Z9XFiZ7uapyDxrh9UskZSmPGa-nWr7mCqzevw/edit#gid=0) lists a collection of equations and formulas for use in DiceCloud. Note that this is community-created and has not been fully tested and verified by the Demiplane staff. Use at your own risk.
